structure.title: FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE
structure.summary:
# Summary of Johnson & Johnson Q2 2023 Earnings Release

## Key Financial Performance
- Reported sales growth of 6.3% to $25.5 billion in Q2 2023
- Operational growth of 7.5% with adjusted operational growth of 6.2%
- Operational growth excluding COVID-19 vaccine reached 8.9%
- Diluted EPS increased 8.9% to $1.96; adjusted EPS increased 8.1% to $2.80
- Net earnings grew 6.9% to $5.144 billion

## Guidance and Strategic Initiatives
- Company raising 2023 full-year guidance midpoints for adjusted operational sales (excluding COVID-19 vaccine) and adjusted operational EPS
- Johnson & Johnson plans to execute a "split off" of Kenvue shares through an exchange offer as the next step in separation, subject to market conditions
- Company positioning itself to become a two-sector company focused on Pharmaceutical and MedTech innovation

## Executive Commentary
- CEO Joaquin Duato highlighted strong performance in Q2 and first half of 2023
- Company entering second half of 2023 from a position of strength with multiple catalysts ahead

structure.title: OVERALL FINANCIAL RESULTS

## Guidance and Strategic Initiatives

structure.title: REGIONAL SALES RESULTS
structure.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This document presents Q2 financial performance data for a healthcare/pharmaceutical company, comparing 2023 results to 2022. The main points covered include:

## Regional Sales Results
- **U.S. Sales**: $13.4 billion in 2023, up 10.2% from $12.2 billion in 2022
- **International Sales**: $12.1 billion in 2023, up 2.2% reported (4.7% operational growth), with currency headwinds of 2.5%
- **Worldwide Total**: $25.5 billion in 2023, up 6.3% reported (7.5% operational growth)

## Segment Sales Results
- **Consumer Health**: $4.0 billion, up 5.4% reported (7.7% operational growth)
- **Pharmaceutical**: $13.7 billion, up 3.1% reported (3.8% operational growth)
- **MedTech**: $7.8 billion, up 12.9% reported (14.7% operational growth), representing the strongest performing segment

## Key Metrics
The document uses both reported and operational metrics, adjusting for currency impacts and acquisitions/divestitures to provide a clearer view of underlying business performance. Currency headwinds negatively impacted reported results across all regions and segments.

structure.title: SEGMENT SALES RESULTS
structure.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This document presents Q2 financial performance data for a healthcare/pharmaceutical company, comparing 2023 results to 2022 across two main analytical frameworks:

## Regional Sales Results
- **U.S. Sales**: $13.4 billion in 2023, up 10.2% from $12.2 billion in 2022
- **International Sales**: $12.1 billion in 2023, up 2.2% reported but 4.7% operationally, with currency headwinds of (2.5%)
- **Worldwide Total**: $25.5 billion in 2023, representing 6.3% reported growth and 7.5% operational growth

## Segment Sales Results
The company operates across three business segments:
- **Consumer Health**: $4.0 billion (5.4% reported growth, 7.7% operational growth)
- **Pharmaceutical**: $13.7 billion (3.1% reported growth, 3.8% operational growth) - largest segment
- **MedTech**: $7.8 billion (12.9% reported growth, 14.7% operational growth) - strongest performer

## Key Metrics
The document uses multiple growth measurements including reported growth, operational growth (excluding currency impacts), and currency-adjusted operational growth (excluding acquisitions/divestitures). Currency translation negatively impacted overall results by approximately 1.2% worldwide, with the U.S. showing no currency impact while international markets faced 2.5% headwinds.

structure.title: SECOND QUARTER 2023 SEGMENT COMMENTARY
structure.summary:
# Second Quarter 2023 Segment Commentary Summary

This document provides an overview of Johnson & Johnson's operational performance across three main business segments in Q2 2023:

## Key Points:

**Pharmaceutical Segment:**
- Achieved 3.9% adjusted operational sales growth worldwide (6.2% excluding COVID-19 Vaccine)
- Growth driven by oncology drugs (DARZALEX, ERLEADA, CARVYKTI), immunology products (STELARA, TREMFYA), pulmonary hypertension medications (UPTRAVI, OPSUMIT), and neuroscience drug (SPRAVATO)
- Partially offset by declines in ZYTIGA, IMBRUVICA, COVID-19 Vaccine, and REMICADE

**MedTech Segment:**
- Achieved 9.9% adjusted operational sales growth worldwide
- Primary growth drivers: electrophysiology products, trauma products, wound closure products, biosurgery, and contact lenses
- Overall operational sales grew 14.7% with Abiomed acquisition contributing 4.8%

**Consumer Health Segment:**
- Achieved 7.7% adjusted operational sales growth worldwide
- Growth primarily driven by OTC products including TYLENOL, MOTRIN, upper respiratory products, smoking cessation products, IMODIUM, NEUTROGENA, and Women's Health products

The document notes that adjusted operational sales exclude the net impact of acquisitions, divestitures, and translational currency effects.

structure.title: NOTABLE NEW ANNOUNCEMENTS IN THE QUARTER
structure.summary:
# Second Quarter 2023 Segment Commentary - Main Points

## Segment Performance
- **Pharmaceutical**: Worldwide adjusted operational sales grew 3.9% (6.2% excluding COVID-19 vaccine), driven by oncology drugs (DARZALEX, ERLEADA, CARVYKTI), immunology products (STELARA, TREMFYA), pulmonary hypertension treatments (UPTRAVI, OPSUMIT), and neuroscience drug SPRAVATO, partially offset by declines in ZYTIGA, IMBRUVICA, and COVID-19 vaccine sales.

- **MedTech**: Worldwide adjusted operational sales grew 9.9%, led by electrophysiology products, trauma products, wound closure, biosurgery, and contact lenses. Overall operational sales grew 14.7% with Abiomed acquisition contributing 4.8%.

- **Consumer Health**: Worldwide adjusted operational sales increased 7.7%, primarily driven by OTC products including TYLENOL, MOTRIN, upper respiratory products, smoking cessation products, IMODIUM, NEUTROGENA, and women's health products.

## Notable Regulatory and Clinical Announcements
Key highlights include first worldwide approval of AKEEGA for metastatic prostate cancer, FDA Fast Track designation for Milvexian, new drug applications for combination therapies, positive Phase 2/3 clinical trial results for multiple pipeline candidates (TAR-200, TREMFYA, nipocalimab, JNJ-2113, RYBREVANT), and advancement of next-generation CAR-T therapies through collaboration with Cellular Biomedicine Group.

nodes.title: Regulatory
nodes.summary:
# Summary of Main Points

This document covers recent regulatory approvals, clinical trial results, and business developments from Janssen Pharmaceuticals:

**Regulatory Approvals:**
- AKEEGA (niraparib and abiraterone acetate) received EC authorization for metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer with BRCA1/2 mutations
- Milvexian granted FDA Fast Track designation for three indications (ischemic stroke, acute coronary syndrome, atrial fibrillation)
- New Drug Application submitted for macitentan and tadalafil combination therapy for pulmonary arterial hypertension
- Supplemental Biologics License Application submitted for CARVYKTI for relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma

**Clinical Trial Results:**
- Phase 2 SunRISe-1 study results for TAR-200 and cetrelimab in bladder cancer
- Phase 3 TREMFYA (guselkumab) positive induction results in ulcerative colitis
- Phase 2 data for nipocalimab in hemolytic disease of the fetus and newborn
- Positive topline results for JNJ-2113 (IL-23 receptor antagonist) in plaque psoriasis
- RYBREVANT (amivantamab-vmjw) plus chemotherapy showed significant improvement in progression-free survival for EGFR exon 20 insertion mutation-positive lung cancer

**Other Developments:**
- Launch of Kenvue Inc. IPO roadshow
- Worldwide collaboration agreement with Cellular Biomedicine Group for next-generation CAR-T therapies
- Over 90 presentations at ASCO and EHA conferences showcasing cancer care advances

structure.title: FULL-YEAR 2023 GUIDANCE
structure.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This document presents Johnson & Johnson's full-year 2023 financial guidance with two sets of projections (July 2023 and April 2023 updates). The main points covered include:

1. **Explanation of Non-GAAP Measures**: J&J clarifies why it does not provide GAAP forward-looking guidance due to the unpredictability of legal proceedings, unusual gains/losses, acquisition expenses, and fair value adjustments.

2. **Key Financial Guidance Metrics**:
   - Adjusted Operational Sales growth projections (6.0-7.0% for July vs. 4.5-5.5% for April)
   - Operational Sales ranges (approximately $99.3B-$100.3B for July vs. $97.9B-$98.9B for April)
   - Estimated Reported Sales figures
   - Adjusted Operational EPS (Diluted) guidance ($10.60-$10.70 for July vs. $10.50-$10.60 for April)
   - Adjusted EPS (Diluted) projections ($10.70-$10.80 for July vs. $10.60-$10.70 for April)

3. **Important Exclusions**: All guidance excludes COVID-19 vaccine sales, acquisition/divestiture impacts, currency translation effects, and intangible amortization expenses.

4. **Webcast Information**: Details on the investor conference call scheduled for 8:30 a.m. Eastern Time, with webcast access and replay availability provided.

structure.title: WEBCAST INFORMATION
structure.summary:
# Summary of Johnson & Johnson Full-Year 2023 Guidance

This document presents Johnson & Johnson's full-year 2023 financial guidance with updates from July 2023 compared to April 2023 projections. The main points covered include:

## Key Financial Metrics:
- **Adjusted Operational Sales Growth**: Increased to 6.0%-7.0% (mid-point 6.5%) in July from 4.5%-5.5% in April
- **Operational Sales**: Projected at $99.3B-$100.3B (mid-point $99.8B) with 7.0%-8.0% growth
- **Reported Sales**: Estimated at $98.8B-$99.8B (mid-point $99.3B) with 6.5%-7.5% growth
- **Adjusted Operational EPS**: Guided at $10.60-$10.70 (mid-point $10.65) with 4.5%-5.5% growth
- **Adjusted EPS**: Projected at $10.70-$10.80 (mid-point $10.75) with 5.5%-6.5% growth

## Important Disclaimers:
- The company does not provide GAAP forward-looking measures due to inability to predict legal proceedings, unusual gains/losses, and acquisition-related expenses with reasonable certainty
- Multiple metrics are non-GAAP measures excluding acquisitions, divestitures, currency impacts, and intangible amortization
- Guidance excludes COVID-19 vaccine sales
- Currency calculations use specific Euro exchange rates for illustrative purposes

## Additional Information:
- Investor conference call scheduled for 8:30 a.m. Eastern Time with webcast access
- Replay and podcast available on the company website

structure.title: ABOUT JOHNSON & JOHNSON
structure.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This partial document contains three main sections from a Johnson & Johnson corporate communication:

1. **Company Overview (About Johnson & Johnson)**: Describes J&J's 135+ year history and position as the world's largest diversified healthcare company. Emphasizes the company's commitment to improving health access, affordability, and creating healthier communities globally through innovation and science.

2. **Non-GAAP Financial Measures**: Explains the use of non-GAAP financial metrics including operational sales growth, adjusted operational sales growth, adjusted net earnings, and adjusted diluted earnings per share. Clarifies that these measures exclude currency impacts, acquisition/divestiture effects, and intangible amortization. References where reconciliations and detailed financial schedules can be found on the company's investor website.

3. **Forward-Looking Statements Disclaimer**: Provides a cautionary note to investors regarding forward-looking statements in the document, including those about future financial performance, product development, market position, and the anticipated separation of the Consumer Health business. Warns that actual results may differ materially from projections due to various risks and uncertainties such as economic factors, competition, and technological changes.

structure.title: NON-GAAP FINANCIAL MEASURES
structure.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This partial document contains three main sections from a Johnson & Johnson corporate communication:

1. **Company Overview**: Describes Johnson & Johnson's mission and values, highlighting its 135+ year history as the world's largest diversified healthcare company. Emphasizes commitment to improving health access, affordability, and creating healthier communities globally.

2. **Non-GAAP Financial Measures**: Explains the use of non-GAAP financial metrics including operational sales growth, adjusted operational sales growth, adjusted net earnings, and adjusted diluted earnings per share. Clarifies that these measures exclude currency impacts, acquisition/divestiture effects, and intangible amortization expenses, and directs readers to reconciliations available on the company website.

3. **Forward-Looking Statements Disclaimer**: Provides a cautionary note to investors regarding forward-looking statements about future financial performance, product development, market position, and business strategy (including the anticipated separation of the Consumer Health business). Warns that actual results may differ materially from projections due to various risks and uncertainties, including economic factors, competition, and technological advances.

structure.title: NOTE TO INVESTORS CONCERNING FORWARD-LOOKING STATEMENTS
structure.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This partial document from Johnson & Johnson contains the following main points:

1. **Company Overview**: Johnson & Johnson's mission statement emphasizing its 135+ year history as the world's largest diversified healthcare company committed to improving health access, affordability, and creating healthier communities.

2. **Non-GAAP Financial Measures**: Explanation of adjusted financial metrics (operational sales growth, adjusted net earnings, adjusted diluted earnings per share) used in reporting, with notes on where reconciliations to GAAP measures can be found.

3. **Forward-Looking Statements Disclaimer**: Comprehensive cautionary note regarding forward-looking statements, including risks and uncertainties such as economic factors, competition, regulatory challenges, patent issues, manufacturing difficulties, litigation, and the planned separation of the Consumer Health business.

4. **Contact Information**: Press and investor contact details for media and investor relations inquiries.

5. **Supplementary Sales Data**: Detailed financial tables presenting quarterly and six-month sales figures broken down by business segment (Consumer Health, Pharmaceutical, MedTech), geography (U.S. and International), with percentage changes in total sales, operational growth, and currency impact. The data includes comparisons between 2023 and 2022 periods, with separate reporting for pharmaceutical sales excluding COVID-19 vaccine revenue.

structure.title: Supplementary Sales Data
structure.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This document contains Johnson & Johnson's supplementary sales data and financial statements for the second quarter and six-month periods of 2023 compared to 2022.

## Main Points Covered:

1. **Sales by Business Segment**: Detailed breakdown of sales for Consumer Health, Pharmaceutical, and MedTech divisions, showing both U.S. and International performance with year-over-year percent changes.

2. **Geographic Sales Analysis**: Sales performance across major geographic regions including the U.S., Europe, Western Hemisphere (excluding U.S.), and Asia-Pacific/Africa, with currency impact analysis.

3. **COVID-19 Vaccine Impact**: Separate reporting of pharmaceutical sales excluding COVID-19 vaccine to show underlying business performance.

4. **Currency Effects**: Detailed tracking of currency impacts on international sales, showing negative currency headwinds of 1.2-2.5% on total sales.

5. **Consolidated Earnings Statement**: Complete income statement showing:
   - Sales and cost of products sold
   - Gross profit margins (67.8% in 2023)
   - Operating expenses (selling, marketing, administrative, and R&D)
   - Net earnings and diluted earnings per share
   - Effective tax rates

6. **Adjusted Non-GAAP Metrics**: Presentation of adjusted earnings before taxes and net earnings, excluding certain items for comparison purposes.

7. **Key Performance Metrics**: Growth rates across segments, with MedTech showing strongest growth (12.9%), while Pharmaceutical showed modest growth (3.1%).

structure.title: Condensed Consolidated Statement of Earnings
structure.summary:
# Description of Johnson & Johnson Condensed Consolidated Statement of Earnings

## Main Points Covered:

**Financial Performance Overview:**
- Presents condensed consolidated earnings statements for Q2 2023 vs. Q2 2022, and six-month periods (2023 vs. 2022)
- Shows both reported and adjusted (non-GAAP) financial metrics

**Key Revenue and Profitability Metrics:**
- Sales to customers increased 6.3% in Q2 2023 ($25.5B) and 6.0% for six months ($50.3B)
- Gross profit margins remained relatively stable at approximately 67-68%
- Net earnings per share (diluted) on reported basis: $1.96 (Q2) and $1.93 (six months)

**Operating Expenses:**
- Cost of products sold, selling/marketing/administrative expenses, and R&D expenses detailed as percentages of sales
- R&D spending maintained at approximately 15% of sales
- Restructuring charges of $145M (Q2) and $275M (six months) related to R&D program prioritization and exits

**Special Items and Adjustments:**
- Significant one-time items including litigation costs, intangible asset amortization, COVID-19 vaccine commitments, and Consumer Health separation costs
- Kenvue IPO impact ($37M non-controlling interest)
- European Medical Device Regulation (MDR) compliance costs

**Adjusted Financial Measures:**
- Adjusted net earnings per share (diluted): $2.80 (Q2) and $5.48 (six months)
- Adjusted earnings provide normalized view excluding special items
- Effective tax rates ranging from 15.8% to 23.9%

structure.title: Reconciliation of Non-GAAP Financial Measures
structure.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This document presents Johnson & Johnson's Non-GAAP financial reconciliation and operational metrics for the second quarter and six-month periods of 2023 compared to 2022.

## Main Points Covered:

**Financial Reconciliation Section:**
- Reconciliation of reported net earnings to adjusted net earnings after tax, showing adjustments for special items
- Key adjustment categories include litigation costs, intangible asset amortization, COVID-19 vaccine-related costs, Consumer Health separation costs, restructuring charges, medical device regulation compliance costs, acquisition/integration costs, and securities gains/losses
- Presentation of adjusted net earnings per share (diluted) on both a reported and operational basis
- Comparison of average diluted shares outstanding between periods

**Special Items Explained:**
- COVID-19 vaccine costs related to contractual commitments and manufacturing network exits
- R&D restructuring expenses ($145M in Q2, $275M YTD) from pharmaceutical segment prioritization, including discontinuation of RSV adult vaccine and other programs
- European Medical Device Regulation (MDR) compliance costs as one-time regulatory expenses
- Tax adjustments and impacts on special items

**Operational Sales Growth Section:**
- Segment-level sales growth analysis (Consumer Health, Pharmaceutical, MedTech) on worldwide, U.S., and international bases
- Comparison of as-reported growth versus operational growth (currency-adjusted)
- Impact of acquisitions and divestitures on growth metrics
- Adjusted operational sales growth showing organic performance across segments

structure.title: Adjusted Operational Sales Growth
structure.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This document presents Johnson & Johnson's reconciliation of Non-GAAP financial measures, specifically focusing on Adjusted Operational Sales Growth across the company's three main business segments: Consumer Health, Pharmaceutical, and MedTech.

## Main Points Covered:

1. **Two Reporting Periods**: The document contains comparative data for Q2 2023 and the first six months of 2023, each compared to their respective 2022 actual figures.

2. **Three Business Segments**: Data is broken down by Consumer Health, Pharmaceutical, and MedTech divisions, with consolidated totals.

3. **Geographic Breakdown**: Sales growth metrics are presented for Worldwide (WW), U.S., and International markets separately.

4. **Reconciliation Components**: The document reconciles reported sales growth to adjusted operational growth by accounting for:
   - Currency impacts (negative adjustments ranging from -0.7% to -3.8%)
   - Abiomed acquisition effects (negative adjustments)
   - Other acquisitions and divestitures (minimal impact)

5. **Key Metrics Presented**:
   - WW As Reported growth rates
   - Currency adjustments
   - WW Operational growth rates
   - WW Adjusted Operational growth rates (final non-GAAP measure)

6. **Performance Trends**: Shows varying performance across segments, with Pharmaceutical and MedTech showing stronger growth in the U.S. versus International markets, while Consumer Health demonstrates more balanced geographic performance.

structure.title: REPORTED SALES vs. PRIOR PERIOD
structure.summary:
# Description of Reported Sales vs. Prior Period Document

## Main Points Covered:

**Document Overview:**
This is a comprehensive financial report comparing sales performance across three major business segments for the second quarter and six-month periods of 2023 versus 2022, with analysis of reported, operational, and currency impacts.

**Three Primary Business Segments:**

1. **Consumer Health Segment** - Includes OTC products, Skin Health/Beauty, Oral Care, Baby Care, Women's Health, and Wound Care/Other categories, with worldwide sales of $4,011MM (Q2 2023) and $7,863MM (six months)

2. **Pharmaceutical Segment** - Comprises Immunology, Infectious Diseases, Neuroscience, Oncology, Pulmonary Hypertension, and Cardiovascular/Metabolism categories, with worldwide sales of $13,731MM (Q2 2023) and $27,144MM (six months)

3. **MedTech Segment** - Includes Interventional Solutions, Orthopaedics, Surgery, and Vision categories, with worldwide sales of $7,788MM (Q2 2023) and $15,269MM (six months)

**Key Metrics Analyzed:**
- Reported sales growth percentages
- Operational growth (excluding currency effects)
- Currency impact on sales
- Geographic breakdown (U.S., International, Worldwide)
- Product-level performance within each category

**Notable Performance Trends:**
- Mixed growth across segments with some products showing strong double-digit growth while others experienced declines
- Currency headwinds impacting international sales
- COVID-19 vaccine sales showing significant decline year-over-year

nodes.title: CONSUMER HEALTH SEGMENT
nodes.summary:
# Document Description

This document presents a comprehensive sales comparison between 2023 and 2022 for two major business segments: Consumer Health and Pharmaceutical. The data is organized by quarter (Second Quarter) and year-to-date (Six Months) periods.

## Main Points Covered:

**Consumer Health Segment:**
- Sales performance across six product categories: OTC, Skin Health/Beauty, Oral Care, Baby Care, Women's Health, and Wound Care/Other
- Each category broken down by US, International (Intl), and Worldwide (WW) markets
- Reported sales figures in millions of dollars with percentage changes
- Operational growth rates and currency impact analysis
- Overall Consumer Health segment showed 5.4% reported growth in Q2 2023 vs. 2022, with 7.7% operational growth offset by 2.3% currency headwinds

**Pharmaceutical Segment:**
- Sales data for major therapeutic areas: Immunology and Infectious Diseases
- Detailed performance metrics for key drugs including Remicade, Simponi/Simponi Aria, Stelara, Tremfya, COVID-19 Vaccine, and others
- Geographic breakdown (US, International, Worldwide) for each product
- Reported, operational, and currency-adjusted growth rates
- Notable trends include Remicade decline (-28.6% WW in Q2), Stelara growth (+7.6% WW in Q2), and significant COVID-19 vaccine sales decline

**Key Metrics:**
- Comparison of reported vs. operational growth rates
- Currency impact quantification
- Year-over-year performance analysis
- Both absolute dollar amounts and percentage changes provided

nodes.title: PHARMACEUTICAL SEGMENT
nodes.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This document presents a comprehensive financial comparison of reported sales versus prior periods for a pharmaceutical and medical technology company, organized by business segments and therapeutic categories.

## Main Points Covered:

**Pharmaceutical Segment Performance:**
- **Immunology**: Mixed results with STELARA and TREMFYA showing growth (7.6% and 18.3% respectively), while REMICADE and SIMPONI/SIMPONI ARIA declined
- **Infectious Diseases**: Overall decline of 14.8% in Q2, with COVID-19 vaccine sales dropping significantly (-47.7%), offset partially by EDURANT growth
- **Neuroscience**: Strong growth of 3.5% in Q2, driven by SPRAVATO (98.2% increase) and CONCERTA (68.2% increase), though INVEGA SUSTENNA declined
- **Oncology**: Robust performance with 8.8% growth, led by DARZALEX (22.4%) and ERLEADA (25.9%), while IMBRUVICA and ZYTIGA declined
- **Pulmonary Hypertension**: Solid growth of 15.3% in Q2, with UPTRAVI and OPSUMIT showing strong double-digit increases
- **Cardiovascular/Metabolism**: Modest growth with XARELTO up 4.7%

**MedTech Segment Performance:**
- **Interventional Solutions**: Exceptional growth of 54.4% in Q2, including new ABIOMED product contribution
- **Electrophysiology**: Strong growth of 23.5% in Q2
- **Orthopaedics**: Steady growth of 5.0% in Q2 across hips, knees, trauma, and spine/sports categories

**Key Metrics Tracked:**
- Reported sales figures in millions of dollars
- Year-over-year comparisons (2023 vs. 2022)
- Operational growth rates (excluding currency effects)
- Currency impact analysis
- Geographic breakdown (US, International, Worldwide)
- Both quarterly (Q2) and six-month period comparisons

nodes.title: MEDTECH SEGMENT
nodes.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This document presents a comprehensive financial analysis of reported sales compared to prior periods, broken down by business segments and geographic regions for the second quarter and six-month periods of 2023 versus 2022.

## Main Points Covered:

**MedTech Segment Performance:**
- Detailed sales data across multiple product categories including Interventional Solutions, Electrophysiology, Abiomed, Orthopaedics (Hips, Knees, Trauma, Spine/Sports), and Surgery (Advanced, General, Vision, Contact Lenses, Surgical)
- Sales metrics presented in millions of dollars with comparisons between 2023 and 2022
- Geographic breakdown into US, International (Intl), and Worldwide (WW) segments

**Key Metrics Analyzed:**
- Reported sales figures
- Operational growth (excluding currency effects)
- Currency impact on sales
- Percentage changes across all categories

**Pharmaceutical Segment Performance:**
- Pharmaceutical sales data with and without COVID-19 vaccine impact
- Separate analysis of COVID-19 vaccine sales trends
- US and International market performance
- Worldwide consolidated results

**Geographic Analysis:**
- Consistent separation of US, International, and Worldwide results
- Currency impact quantification showing negative effects ranging from -0.4% to -6.5%
- Operational growth rates demonstrating underlying business performance

**Overall Company Results:**
- Consolidated worldwide sales combining MedTech and Pharmaceutical segments
- Total company performance metrics for both quarterly and six-month periods
- Supplemental sales reconciliation data

The document emphasizes the distinction between reported growth and operational growth, highlighting the significant impact of currency fluctuations on international sales performance.

structure.title: Supplemental Sales Reconciliation
structure.summary:
# Main Points Covered in the Partial Document

## Sales Performance Analysis
- Detailed breakdown of pharmaceutical sales by geography (U.S. and International) for 2023 vs. 2022
- COVID-19 vaccine sales comparison showing significant decline year-over-year
- Worldwide sales reconciliation excluding COVID-19 vaccine impact
- Percentage changes in sales with operational and currency impact analysis

## Segment Performance
- Income before tax reporting by business segment (Pharmaceutical, MedTech, Consumer Health, and Unallocated)
- Comparison of reported vs. adjusted income before tax metrics
- Percentage of sales analysis for profitability assessment
- Second quarter and six-month period comparisons

## Non-GAAP Adjustments
- Reconciliation of reported to adjusted income before tax
- Detailed breakdown of adjustment items including:
  - Intangible asset amortization
  - Litigation-related costs
  - COVID-19 vaccine-related costs
  - Restructuring charges
  - Acquisition and integration costs
  - Medical device regulation expenses
  - Consumer Health separation costs

## Geographic Performance
- U.S. market performance showing strong growth (9.2-10.6%)
- International market showing mixed results with currency headwinds
- Currency impact quantification on reported results

## Key Metrics
- Adjusted income before tax margins by segment
- Year-over-year growth rates with operational and currency components
- Rounding notes for financial reconciliation accuracy

structure.title: Income Before Tax by Segment
structure.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This document presents Johnson & Johnson's reconciliation of Non-GAAP (Generally Accepted Accounting Principles) financial measures for the second quarter and year-to-date periods of 2023 compared to 2022.

## Main Points Covered:

1. **Income Before Tax by Segment Reconciliation**: Shows reported and adjusted income before tax across four business segments (Consumer Health, Pharmaceutical, MedTech, and Unallocated) with percentage-to-sales metrics for both Q2 quarterly and year-to-date comparisons.

2. **Non-GAAP Adjustments**: Details various adjustments made to convert GAAP figures to Non-GAAP measures, including:
   - Intangible asset amortization expense
   - In-process research and development costs
   - Litigation-related charges
   - COVID-19 vaccine-related costs
   - Loss/gain on securities
   - Restructuring charges
   - Acquisition, integration, and divestiture costs
   - Medical device regulation expenses
   - Consumer Health separation costs

3. **GAAP to Non-GAAP Reconciliation**: Comprehensive reconciliation tables converting GAAP financial metrics to Non-GAAP adjusted metrics across multiple income statement line items including:
   - Cost of products sold
   - Selling, marketing and administrative expenses
   - Research and development expense
   - Other income/expense
   - Provision for taxes
   - Net earnings

4. **Comparative Analysis**: Year-over-year comparisons for both quarterly and six-month periods, showing adjusted profitability improvements and operational performance metrics.

structure.title: GAAP to Non-GAAP Reconciliation
structure.summary:
# Description of Partial Document

This document presents a **GAAP to Non-GAAP Reconciliation** for Johnson & Johnson and Subsidiaries, comparing financial results for the second quarter and six-month periods ending July 2, 2023 versus July 3, 2022.

## Main Points Covered:

1. **Financial Metrics Reconciliation**: The document reconciles GAAP (Generally Accepted Accounting Principles) figures to Non-GAAP adjusted figures across multiple expense and income categories.

2. **Key Line Items Adjusted**:
   - Cost of products sold
   - Selling, marketing and administrative expenses
   - Research and development expenses
   - Other income/expense
   - Interest income/expense
   - Provision for taxes on income
   - Net earnings

3. **Adjustment Categories**: Various non-recurring or non-operational items are excluded from GAAP results, including:
   - Intangible asset amortization
   - Litigation-related costs
   - Restructuring charges
   - Medical device regulation costs
   - Consumer Health separation costs and taxes
   - Acquisition, integration, and divestiture-related costs
   - In-process research and development
   - COVID-19 vaccine-related costs
   - Tax legislation and other tax-related items
   - Loss/gain on securities

4. **Comparative Analysis**: Side-by-side comparison of quarterly and year-to-date results showing both GAAP and Non-GAAP metrics for performance evaluation.
